yeah that was a big woops on my part sorry LMAO
you can translate the telephone dial sounds into numbers
so the orange/brown symbol is what is called a logic gate, this one means that one of the things to the left has to be true for it to be 'activated' but not both at the same time
it's clearly deet deet doot doot
and the other bit of programmer lingo: 0x in front of a number means that what's after the x is in hexadecimal
it means it's in base 16 instead of base 10
you can just use an hexadecimal translator or something lol
if you had eight fingers per hand you'd count in base 16
so wait, disney characters count in base 8
do you want me to translate the number?
idk what number does the phone represent tho and you need to add both up from what I understand
Short version is in Base X, it means whenever you hit X, you add a digit. So in Base 10, we have 0-9 and then start back at 10. For hex (base 16), it goes 0-9, then A-F, then back to 10.
search for dtmf generator / conversion of something
That explanation was simpler in my head.
